identity.name: Stock Buy Opportunity Scanner
identity.description: Monitors a watchlist of stocks across multiple signals — technical, fundamental, sentiment, and macro context — and surfaces high-conviction buy opportunities with a structured thesis and risk assessment. Does not execute trades. Alerts the user with enough context to make an informed decision quickly.
identity.tags: stocks, equity, technical-analysis, fundamental, sentiment, buy-signal

goal.statement: Identify stocks on the watchlist where multiple independent signals converge to suggest a high-probability buy entry point — producing a concise, structured thesis the user can evaluate in under 60 seconds and act on with confidence. Prioritize precision over recall: fewer, better alerts.

success_criteria.criterion: At least 3 of the 5 signal categories (technical, fundamental, sentiment, catalyst, macro) must independently suggest a buy — not just one strong signal overriding the others.
The generated thesis is specific to this stock at this moment — not generic boilerplate. It names the exact trigger, the entry rationale, and the key risk.
Downside scenario is clearly identified with a specific invalidation condition (e.g. "thesis is invalidated if price breaks below $X" or "if Q2 earnings miss guidance by more than 5%").

Over a rolling 30-day window, alerted opportunities outperform the S&P 500 over the subsequent 10 trading days, or are rated useful by the user via feedback. (Ground truth used once available; judge LLM proxy until then.)

input.transform: compute: rsi_14, macd_signal, sma_20, sma_50, sma_200, distance_from_52w_high_pct, distance_from_52w_low_pct, avg_volume_20d, today_volume, volume_ratio, bollinger_position
compute: pe_ratio, forward_pe, peg_ratio, revenue_growth_yoy, earnings_growth_yoy, free_cash_flow_margin, debt_to_equity, gross_margin_trend

fallback.query: US market macro environment Fed rates today {date}

stage.system: You are a quantitative analyst. Score each signal category independently. Do not let one strong signal bias the others. Return only valid JSON.
You are a portfolio manager writing a concise buy thesis for a colleague. Be specific. Name exact levels, triggers, and timeframes. No boilerplate. No hedging language. Return only valid JSON.
You are a risk manager stress-testing a buy thesis. Be adversarial. Identify the specific conditions that would prove the thesis wrong. Return only valid JSON.

child.pass_fields: ticker, current_price, thesis_generation, risk_assessment, signal_scoring.weighted_score, signal_scoring.signals

constraints.must_preserve: output JSON schema for all three stages

eval_set.filter: judge_score IS NOT NULL AND (ground_truth_10d_return IS NOT NULL OR alert_sent=false)
